description: Multi-state part-year fixture: Single filer moves PA -> IL mid-year TY2025. PA resident Jan 1 - Jun 30 (181 days), IL resident Jul 1 - Dec 31 (184 days). Two W-2s: Philly Corp ($30k, $2,500 fed wh, $921 PA wh) and Chicago LLC ($35k, $4,000 fed wh, $1,732.50 IL wh). Standard deduction, no dependents, no OBBBA triggers, no itemized, no Schedule C/E. This is the FIRST golden fixture that exercises the state plugin dispatch layer end-to-end. WAVE 6 UPGRADE: PA and IL now source wages from W-2 state_rows[] (box 16) instead of day-proration of the resident-basis tax. PA taxes only the $30k Philly-sourced wages at 3.07% = $921 exactly; IL taxes only the $35k Chicago-sourced wages, less the $2,850 personal exemption, at 4.95% = $1,591.43.
authority: skill/scripts/states/pa.py — PA flat 3.07% on tenforty PA-40 base; v1 nonresident/part-year uses days_in_state/365 proration of resident-basis tax

skill/scripts/states/il.py — IL flat 4.95% on (federal AGI + Sch M adds - Sch M subs) - exemption; TY2025 personal exemption $2,850; v1 nonresident/part-year uses days_in_state/365 proration of resident-basis tax

skill/reference/state-reciprocity.json — PA reciprocity partners {IN, MD, NJ, OH, VA, WV}; IL reciprocity partners {IA, KY, MI, WI}; PA<->IL is NOT reciprocal so both states tax the income earned while a resident
test_golden_simple_w2.py locks Single $65k W-2 -> $5,755 federal income tax for TY2025 (same federal totals as this fixture: $65k AGI, $15,750 std ded, $49,250 TI)

federal_hand_check.agi_calc: no adjustments to income -> AGI = total_income = 65000
federal_hand_check.federal_income_tax: Locked to simple_w2_standard golden: IRS tax tables Single TY2025 -> 5755 at TI 49250 (10% * 11925 + 12% * 36550 + 22% * 800 = 5754.50 -> 5755 via $50 tax-table midpoint rounding)

pa_hand_check.pa_compensation_base: PA's PA-40 starts from 8 income classes. tenforty's resident-basis calc sees the full $65,000 canonical w2_income, but the Wave 6 PA plugin branches on W-2 state rows: state_rows[0].state == 'PA' with state_wages == $30,000, so PA-sourced wages = $30,000 exactly.
pa_hand_check.pa_resident_basis_tax_calc: 65000 * 0.0307 = 1995.50 (full-year resident hypothetical, stamped in state_total_tax_resident_basis for regression)
pa_hand_check.pa_sourced_tax_calc: 30000 * 0.0307 = 921.00 exactly (Wave 6: the PA plugin computes class 1 tax on PA-sourced wages directly instead of day-prorating the resident-basis tax)

il_hand_check.il_base_income_calc: federal AGI 65000 + Sch M additions 0 - Sch M subtractions 0 = 65000 (stamped in state_base_income_approx / state_base_income_after_adjustments for the resident hypothetical)
il_hand_check.il_additions_detail: 0 — no 1099-INT box 8, no 1099-DIV box 11 (no muni interest)
il_hand_check.il_subtractions_detail: 0 — no SSA-1099 box 5, no 1099-R box 2a, no 1099-INT box 3 (no Treasury interest)
il_hand_check.il_sourced_wages: 35000 — sum of state_rows[IL].state_wages across all W-2s

WAVE_6_SOURCING_DELIVERY.scope: Wave 6 delivered real W-2 state-row sourcing for the wage side of nonresident / part-year PA and IL returns. The plugins now inspect W2.state_rows[] and, when a row matches the plugin's state code, compute the state tax on the sourced wage amount directly instead of day-prorating the resident-basis tax.
WAVE_6_SOURCING_DELIVERY.pa_sourced_logic: PA plugin: when at least one W-2 state row has state_code='PA', PA tax = sum(state_rows[PA].state_wages) * 0.0307. For this fixture that's 30000 * 0.0307 = 921.00 exactly, which matches the Philly Corp box 17 withholding and produces a zero-owed / zero-refund position.
WAVE_6_SOURCING_DELIVERY.il_sourced_logic: IL plugin: when at least one W-2 state row has state_code='IL', IL sourced base = sum(state_rows[IL].state_wages) + sum(Schedule C net for business_location_state='IL'), IL sourced taxable = max(0, sourced_base - exemption_total), IL tax = sourced_taxable * 0.0495. For this fixture that's (35000 - 2850) * 0.0495 = 1591.4250 -> 1591.43 with ROUND_HALF_UP.
WAVE_6_SOURCING_DELIVERY.still_open: IL additions/subtractions (Line 2 muni addback, Line 5 SS/retirement, Sch M Line 22 Treasury) are NOT applied on the sourced path in v1 — they only affect the resident-basis hypothetical stamped as state_total_tax_resident_basis. A follow-up can fold the sourced line items through those adds/subs once a per-state 1099 sourcing rule lands.
WAVE_6_SOURCING_DELIVERY.resident_basis_preserved: The resident-basis tax is still stamped under state_total_tax_resident_basis so regression tests can compare the Wave 6 sourced answer against the Wave 5 day-prorated hypothetical.

TODO_il_adds_subs_no_op.note: CP8-D added IL-1040 Line 2 muni-interest addback, IL-1040 Line 5 Social Security + qualified retirement subtraction, and IL Schedule M Step 2 Line 22 U.S. Treasury subtraction. This fixture has ZERO 1099-INT, ZERO 1099-DIV, ZERO SSA-1099, and ZERO 1099-R, so all CP8-D adds/subs evaluate to 0 and the IL base income equals federal AGI. Verify by reading expected_il_state_return.state_specific.il_additions_total and il_subtractions_total — both must be '0.00'. A future fixture should exercise the CP8-D adds/subs with non-zero muni and Treasury interest.
